L1.area: Scene description file parsing and layer data loading

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in binary scene file parsing where a crafted file containing invalid child prim entries can lead to memory being accessed after it has been freed when the application later iterates over those children.

L3.area: Binary crate format data population and prim spec processing within the scene description framework
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the binary crate file loading logic where a crafted crate file containing a primChildren field with invalid entries (such as empty names, names with invalid characters, or duplicate entries) on a prim can cause memory to be accessed after it has been freed when the application later accesses that prim's children.

ground_truth.reason: A crafted binary crate file with invalid primChildren entries (empty names, invalid characters, or duplicates) causes a use-after-free when the application later accesses those children, as the entries reference invalid or freed memory.
